text: Managing a team well requires several important skills. First, clear communication is essential. Everyone needs to understand their tasks and the team’s goals. Second, delegation means sharing work fairly, not doing everything yourself. This builds trust and helps others grow. Third, giving regular feedback helps team members improve and feel valued. However, problems can happen, so conflict resolution is key. When disagreements arise, listen to both sides and find a solution together. Finally, keep motivation high by celebrating successes and showing appreciation. A good manager balances these skills to create a positive, productive team environment where everyone feels heard and supported.
